ASBESTOS SETTLEMENT CALENDAR

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, Advancing Current Trial Date; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jan-05-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party James Murray
D. Paul Bird II of DENTONS US LLP (415-267-4075), appearing for defendant / opposing party J. T. Thorpe & Son, Inc.
Anthony T. Topolsky of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, ADVANCING CURRENT TRIAL DATE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiff's motion to advance the trial date with preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The August 1, 2016 trial date is advanced to April 25, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. The 120th day is April 28, 2016 (as stated in this Court's tentative ruling posted January 29, 2015, if the motion is granted, the 120th day will be calculated based on the scheduled hearing date of December 30, 2015). The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 21, 2016. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than February 26, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 11, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by April 18, 2016 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on April 21, 2016. All parties including plaintiff and all carriers must be present at the settlement conference. Fact discovery cut-off date is April 8, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 22, 2016. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than January 29, 2016. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than January 11, 2016 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than January 15, 2016 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Plaintiff is to make every effect to produce his witnesses for depositions by February 5, 2016. (Mini Minutes continued, see next entry.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE

No objection made and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiff's motion to advance the trial date with preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is advanced from August 24, 2015 to June 8, 2015 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day of trial is June 10, 2015. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. Last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 3, 2015. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice dictated by the C.C.P., unless plaintiff stipulates otherwise. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Before the Court will hear any discovery motion the parties involved in the discovery dispute must schedule and attend an informal discovery conference with the Court. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than April 24, 2015. Responses to the demand are due no later than May 1, 2015. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by June 1, 2015 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on June 4, 2015. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 22, 2015. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 5, 2015. No later than February 24, 2015 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than February 24, 2015 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on May 6, 2015 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on June 8, 2015 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by June 4, 2015 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
